title: 22nd Run for the Rose 5K returns to Helix Park as storms threaten Texas Medical Center
content_md:
The 22nd Annual Run for the Rose 5K was held on Sunday morning, April 12, 2026, at Helix Park in the Texas Medical Center, drawing runners, walkers, brain-tumor survivors, and families to the Dr. Marnie Rose Foundation's signature event.

The morning's program included a chip-timed 5K Run, a 5K Walk, a Family 1K, and a Survivor Stroll for brain tumor patients and their support teams. The 5K offered overall awards for top male and female finishers as well as age-group prizes. The foundation, established in memory of Dr. Marnie Rose — a young pediatrician featured on ABC's Houston Medical who died of glioblastoma — directs proceeds toward brain cancer research, pediatric initiatives, and patient support, in partnership with MD Anderson Cancer Center and Texas Children's Hospital.

Weather at the start was warm, very humid, and increasingly unsettled. Houston-area observations showed temperatures near 72°F at the early-morning start, partly cloudy skies giving way to mostly cloudy conditions, dew points near 70°F for humidity around 90 percent, and light winds. The high for the day reached 79°F. The National Weather Service issued a Flash Flood Warning for Harris and southern Waller counties later in the day, with three to four inches of rain having already fallen in parts of the metro area and an additional one to three inches expected. The Texas Medical Center course stayed in the dry early window before storms ramped up after midday.

The Run for the Rose has become one of the Texas Medical Center's defining community events, regularly drawing thousands of participants and hundreds of brain tumor survivors. The 2026 edition marked more than two decades of the foundation's annual gathering at the institution that treats so many of the patients its programs are designed to help.

title: Saint Arnold Art Car IPA 5K runs from Lyons Avenue brewery on rainy Sunday morning
content_md:
The Saint Arnold Art Car IPA 5K presented by UHD was held on Sunday morning, April 12, 2026, starting and finishing at Saint Arnold Brewing Company at 2000 Lyons Avenue in Houston's near-northeast neighborhoods.

The race ties into the Art Car IPA brand and the broader Houston Art Car community, and traditionally includes a costume and creative-attire element. Post-race, finishers gathered at the Saint Arnold beer garden and restaurant, a long-running anchor for Houston craft beer culture and one of the more recognizable race-finish venues in the city.

Morning weather was warm, very humid, and trending unsettled. Observations from Houston-area stations showed temperatures near 72°F at the early start, partly cloudy skies giving way to mostly cloudy by mid-morning, dew points near 70°F for humidity in the high 80s to low 90s, and light winds. The day's high reached 79°F. A Flash Flood Warning was later issued for Harris County, with three to four inches of rain having already fallen and another one to three inches expected; the heaviest storm activity held off until after midday, allowing the early 5K window to run before the worst of it.

The Art Car IPA 5K is one of the longer-running spring fixtures on the Houston race calendar and continues to draw a mix of competitive runners and a strong recreational field oriented toward the post-race gathering.

title: Vintage Park 13.1 half marathon and 5K go off ahead of Sunday's Houston flash flooding
content_md:
The Vintage Park 13.1 Half Marathon, presented by Houston Methodist, was held on Sunday morning, April 12, 2026, at St. Luke's Health – The Vintage Hospital, 20171 Chasewood Park Drive, in northwest Houston. The morning featured a half marathon, a 5K, a half marathon relay, a wheelchair division, an Ainsley's Angels 5K and half marathon, and a Kids 1K.

The half marathon and 5K both started at 7:00 AM, with the Kids 1K following at 9:30 AM. The course wound through the Vintage Park district and surrounding streets in the Champions / Spring area. Finishers received a custom race shirt, bib, finisher medal, and post-race tacos from Chuy's, plus downloadable course photos.

Weather at the 7:00 AM start was warm, very humid, and increasingly threatening. Northwest Houston observations showed temperatures near 72°F at the gun, broken to mostly cloudy skies, dew points near 70°F for humidity around 90 percent, and light winds. The day's high reached 79°F. The early start time was a meaningful tailwind for the field: a Flash Flood Warning was later issued for Harris and southern Waller counties, with three to four inches of rain already on the ground and another one to three inches expected, and severe storms became more likely after about 2:00 PM. The half marathon field was largely off the course before the heaviest weather moved in.

The Vintage Park 13.1 is a long-standing fixture on the northwest-Houston spring calendar and serves as both a competitive half marathon and a charity-friendly community event with the Ainsley's Angels divisions.

title: Krystine Beneke wins women's 50 miler at Brazos Bend as humid morning gives way to scattered storms
content_md:
Trail Racing Over Texas hosted the spring edition of the Brazos Bend 50 at Brazos Bend State Park in Needville on Saturday, April 11, 2026, with distances ranging from 5K to 50 miles on one of the flattest trail courses in the state.

The 50-mile event started at 6:00 AM under headlamps, with the 50K following at 7:00 AM, the 25K at 7:30 AM, the 10K at 8:00 AM, and the 5K at 8:30 AM. The finish line remained open until 9:00 PM, giving ultrarunners a full 15 hours to complete the 50-mile distance.

Krystine Beneke won the women's 50-mile race, a result picked up by iRunFar in its weekly trail and ultra recap. Complete results across the six distances — 50 mile, 50K, 25K, 10K, 5K, and Kids Run — were posted on the Trail Racing Over Texas results page following the event.

Weather at Brazos Bend mirrored conditions across the greater Houston area: a warm, very humid morning with temperatures near 71-72°F at the early starts, broken cloud cover, and dew points sitting just below the air temperature for relative humidity in the high 80s to low 90s. Scattered showers and storms moved through southeast Texas during the day, with the strongest cells west and northwest of Houston, and isolated rain reaching the park. Conditions were typical of a humid spring day on the prairie — challenging for the longer distances but not extreme.

The course traverses hard crushed rock, soft hiking trails under tree canopy, and horse trails through the park's 5,000 acres. Brazos Bend is famous for its resident alligator population, and runners frequently report spotting the reptiles sunning themselves along the levees that border the park's lakes. The course is 99.9% flat with only a single 10-foot rise, making it a popular choice for first-time ultra runners and those chasing fast times on dirt.

The 2026 50K served as the RRCA Texas State Championships, drawing competitive trail runners from across the state. Trail Racing Over Texas hosts multiple events at Brazos Bend throughout the year, and the post-race taco tradition at the finish-line tent continues to be a staple of the southeast Texas ultra running community.

title: 30th Bellaire Trolley Run goes off in muggy morning conditions as HARRA Spring Series 5K
content_md:
The 30th Annual Bellaire Trolley Run returned to Bellaire City Hall on Saturday, April 11, 2026, marking three decades of the inner-loop suburb's signature community running event. The race served as the next stop on the HARRA Spring Series schedule.

The morning began with the 1-mile division at 7:30 AM, followed by the wheelchair 5K at 7:50 AM, the timed 5K at 8:00 AM, and the stroller 5K at 8:10 AM. The race started and finished in front of Bellaire City Hall at 7008 South Rice Avenue, winding through the tree-lined residential streets of one of Houston's most walkable neighborhoods.

Conditions at the start were warm and very humid, typical of a Houston spring morning before a frontal pattern. Temperatures sat near 71-72°F at the 7:30 to 8:00 AM gun times with broken-to-overcast skies, light north-to-northeast winds around 7-9 mph, and humidity in the high 80s to low 90s. Scattered showers and storms were tracking across southeast Texas during the day, with the heaviest activity west and northwest of the metro area, but the inner-Loop course stayed largely dry through the morning races.

Organized by the PATRONS for Bellaire Parks nonprofit, the Trolley Run supports local parks preservation and has become a beloved spring tradition in the Bellaire community. All finishers received a medal and T-shirt, and the post-race party featured vendors offering treats and giveaways.

The inclusion of wheelchair and stroller divisions alongside the traditional timed 5K reflects the event's commitment to inclusivity, welcoming participants of all abilities and family configurations to the starting line.

title: Race for the Dome circles the Astrodome under humid skies on its 6th running
content_md:
The 6th Annual Race for the Dome returned to NRG Park on Saturday, April 11, 2026, celebrating the Astrodome's 61st birthday with a 5K road course through the park grounds and a Kids 1K fun run.

Participants were encouraged to wear vintage gear or Dome attire as they ran a course that crossed the bridge over Kirby Drive and circled the iconic Astrodome, one of Houston's most recognizable landmarks. The event drew a mix of runners and Houston history enthusiasts united by their affection for the stadium that once hosted the Houston Astros, Oilers, and Livestock Show and Rodeo.

Weather at the morning start was warm and muggy. Houston-area observations showed temperatures in the low 70s, broken cloud cover, dew points in the high 60s for humidity in the high 80s to low 90s, and light winds. Scattered storms tracked through southeast Texas during the day, with the strongest activity west and northwest of the metro core, but the NRG Park course stayed largely dry through the early morning race window.

All proceeds benefited the Astrodome Conservancy, the nonprofit working to preserve and repurpose the historic structure. The conservancy has led efforts to find a sustainable future for the Dome, which was designated a State Antiquities Landmark in 2017.

The Race for the Dome has become an annual tradition for runners who want to combine their miles with a dose of Houston nostalgia, running in the shadow of what was once called the Eighth Wonder of the World.

title: Spring Fling Houston brings 5K, 10K, and Bigfoot trophies to Cullen Park
content_md:
Bigfoot Running Company hosted the 2026 Spring Fling - Houston at Cullen Park in far west Houston on Saturday, April 11, 2026, offering 1 Mile, 5K, and 10K options on the park's mixed-surface trails.

The day opened with packet pickup from 7:30 to 8:45 AM, followed by a Kids Dash at 8:55 AM and the National Anthem and main race start at 9:00 AM. The 5K and 10K courses wound through Cullen Park's pavement, crushed granite, and dirt paths, with aid stations on course and a Race Rally Tent for snacks and refreshments at the finish.

Weather at the 9:00 AM start was warm and very humid for early April. Houston-area observations showed temperatures climbing through the mid-70s by the gun, broken cloud cover, dew points in the upper 60s, and light winds. Scattered storms were active across southeast Texas through the day, with the strongest cells west and northwest of the metro area and isolated showers possible later in the morning. The conditions were typical for a spring morning in Houston — manageable for the 5K but a noticeable factor for the 10K field.

Finishers across all distances received custom Spring Fling medals and event shirts, and Bigfoot Trophy awards went to the top finishers. The series brands itself around the Bigfoot mascot and lean-in event vibe, with a live DJ at the finish line announcing names as runners crossed.

Cullen Park, on Saums Road in 77084, is a familiar venue for west Houston and Katy-area runners and provides a flat, traffic-free setting with easy parking — a useful combination for a community-style race.

title: Sienna Run for the Rosé 5K supports Child Advocates of Fort Bend in Missouri City
content_md:
The Sienna Run for the Rosé 5K was held on Saturday morning, April 11, 2026, at the Sawmill Event Pavilion in the Sienna master-planned community in Missouri City, with the race window running from 8:00 to 11:00 AM.

Proceeds from the event benefited Child Advocates of Fort Bend, a nonprofit that serves child abuse victims through its Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASA) program and a Children's Advocacy Center. Cash prizes were awarded to the top three overall finishers — $100 for first, $50 for second, and $25 for third — with additional age-group awards.

Weather at the 8:00 AM start was warm and muggy. Observations from Houston-area stations showed temperatures around 71-72°F, broken cloud cover, light north-northeast winds at 7-9 mph, and humidity in the high 80s to low 90s. Scattered showers tracked across southeast Texas during the day, with the heaviest cells west and northwest of the metro area. The course in Missouri City stayed largely dry through the early morning race window, though humidity remained a factor for runners.

Packet pickup ran the evening before at the Sawmill Pavilion and again on race morning. The post-race area featured vendor booths, food trucks, a DJ, and finish-line festivities, in keeping with the family-friendly community-event format that has become a Sienna tradition.

title: AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K returns to Webster on humid Saturday morning
content_md:
The AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K was held in Webster on Saturday, April 11, 2026, with a 5K Run/Walk for adults and a Kids K for children nine and under.

The race draws participants from the Bay Area and southeast Houston suburbs and is timed and produced through the RunSignUp results platform, with results from prior years and prior race dates archived on the same page.

Weather across the Webster and Clear Lake area mirrored the broader Houston-area pattern: warm and muggy at the morning start with temperatures near 71-72°F, broken cloud cover, light winds, and humidity in the high 80s to low 90s. Scattered showers and storms moved through southeast Texas during the day, with the heaviest activity well west and northwest of the metro area, leaving the Webster course largely dry through the morning.

The race remains a community-scale event in Houston's southeast suburbs, on a calendar weekend that featured a notably crowded slate of charity 5Ks across the metro area.

race_name: Project Joy and Hope Fun Run
title: 4th Annual Project Joy and Hope 5K runs at the foot of the San Jacinto Monument
content_md:
The 4th Annual Project Joy and Hope 5K Fun Run was held on Saturday morning, April 11, 2026, at the San Jacinto Monument in La Porte, where runners and walkers gathered for an in-person 5K with a parallel virtual option.

The event supports Project Joy and Hope, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that provides support to seriously ill children and their families in the greater Houston area. Participants received a commemorative t-shirt, post-race snacks and drinks, and a finisher medal. Registration closed the afternoon before the race.

Weather at the start was warm and very humid. Morning observations across the metro area showed temperatures around 71-72°F, broken cloud cover, dew points sitting just below the air temperature for relative humidity in the high 80s to low 90s, and light north-northeast winds. Scattered showers and storms moved through southeast Texas during the day, with the strongest cells west and northwest of Houston, and the San Jacinto site stayed largely dry through the morning race window.

The San Jacinto Monument grounds — site of the 1836 battle that secured Texas independence — provide a rare combination of long flat sightlines and historic surroundings for a 5K, and have become a recurring backdrop for community charity races on the Bay Area side of the metro.

title: Autism Speaks Empower Walk & 5K Run debuts at City Place in Spring
content_md:
The Houston Empower Walk & 5K Run, fueled by Autism Speaks, was held on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at City Place at 1250 Lake Plaza Drive in Spring, with an 8:00 AM start.

The event combined Autism Speaks's signature community walk with a 5K timed run and a Superhero Sprint for younger children, giving families three ways to participate. Funds raised support Autism Speaks programs in research, advocacy, and direct support for autistic individuals and their families.

Morning weather at City Place was warm and muggy. Observations from north-side Houston stations showed temperatures near 71-72°F at the 8:00 AM start, broken cloud cover, dew points in the upper 60s for humidity around 90 percent, and light winds. Scattered storms were active across southeast Texas through the day, with the heaviest cells tracking through areas farther west and northwest, allowing the Spring-area course to stay largely dry through the morning.

City Place — the mixed-use development at Springwoods Village formerly known as ExxonMobil Campus — has become a regular venue for north-side community events, with closed roads, ample parking, and a built-in plaza for post-race vendor and team activities.

title: Yuri's Fun Run returns to Nassau Bay to celebrate space history
content_md:
Runners gathered in Nassau Bay on the morning of Saturday, April 4, 2026 for the annual Yuri's Fun Run Houston, a chip-timed 5K and 10K held at St. Thomas the Apostle Episcopal Church on Upper Bay Road in the Clear Lake community. The Kids 1K went off at 7:15 a.m., followed by the 5K and 10K at 7:35 a.m. Conditions were typical for early April in the Houston area.

The course wound through the streets of Nassau Bay, a small municipality where Apollo-era astronauts, engineers, and flight directors have lived since the early days of the space program. The neighborhood setting gives the race a distinct character among Houston-area road events, with residential streets leading past homes with a direct connection to American spaceflight history.

In the 10K, Virginia Frank crossed the line first among all finishers with a chip time of 40:57 — the fastest overall performance of the morning. Patrick Scott took the top men's spot in 44:13. In the 5K, Richard McEvoy-Kemp won the men's division in 19:20, and Charlotte Maples led the women's field in 21:37. The 5K drew more than 230 finishers across all age groups, with competitive fields stretching from children's divisions through the 85-89 age group.

The race is staged as part of Yuri's Night, a global celebration marking two milestones that both fell on April 12: Yuri Gagarin's first human spaceflight in 1961 and the inaugural launch of the U.S. Space Shuttle in 1981. The Houston edition is scheduled on or near that anniversary each year. Proceeds benefit the Houston Learning Center of the Challenger Center for Space Science Education. The event is part of the Texas 5K Series organized by Running Alliance Sport.

title: 18th annual green6.2 draws Earth Week crowd to CITYCENTRE
content_md:
Houston Running Co. hosted the 18th annual green6.2 at CITYCENTRE on Saturday, April 4, 2026, drawing runners to the mixed-use development at 800 Town and Country Boulevard in the Energy Corridor area for one of the spring season's most consistent local events. The 10K and 5K started at 7:30 a.m., with a kids and family 1K run following at 9:30 a.m.

The race is staged each year around Earth Week and built its identity on sustainability practices from the outset. The 2026 edition continued those commitments with digital race packets, reusable water stations, a medal recycling option, and a shoe drive at the finish line. Organizers encouraged participants to carpool or bike to the event. All participants received a finisher medal and technical t-shirt; age group awards were presented to the top finisher in ten-year brackets for both the 5K and 10K.

The event benefits three local nonprofit organizations, though specific beneficiaries for the 2026 edition were not listed in advance materials reviewed. The race began in 2008 with approximately 200 participants and has grown to more than 2,000 in recent years, making it one of the larger spring road races in Houston. It is produced by Houston Running Co., which also manages the Houston Running Co. Holiday Series.

A virtual race option was offered alongside the in-person distances for participants who could not attend on race day.

title: Third annual PEEPS race fills Energy Corridor on Easter weekend
content_md:
The third annual Running With My PEEPS 5K/10K took place on Saturday, April 4, 2026 at the Addicks Park and Ride on the Katy Freeway in Houston's Energy Corridor district, coinciding with Easter weekend. The Kids Dash started at 8:00 a.m., the 10K at 8:15 a.m., and the 5K at 8:20 a.m.

The course followed a flat, out-and-back route along Park Row Drive adjacent to the Addicks Park and Ride complex, a format the organizers have used since the event's inaugural edition. The race is part of the official PEEPS 5K/10K Run Series, a nationally licensed series tied to the Just Born candy brand. Participants received officially licensed PEEPS finisher medals, performance tech shirts, and participant bibs. Post-race refreshments included PEEPS marshmallow candy.

Festivities at the finish area included a PEEPS chick mascot appearance, an Easter Bunny visit, and a kids' Easter egg hunt station. The event registration was capped at 1,500 participants; prior editions sold out ahead of race day. The race also offered a virtual option for remote participants.

The event is produced independently and is not affiliated with a local running club or charity series, though it operates as part of the broader PEEPS-branded national run series.

title: Always Hope Easter Run stages 5K, 10K, half marathon at Eleanor Tinsley Park
content_md:
The Always Hope Easter Run 5K/10K/13.1 was held on Saturday, April 4, 2026, at Eleanor Tinsley Park, 3600 Allen Parkway in Houston, with a 1-mile Family Fun Run on the same morning program.

The event used a staggered wave structure with the first wave starting at 7:30 AM CDT. Listings described it as a smaller, private-group format with a registration cap per wave, with waves closing as they filled. A virtual-run option was offered alongside the in-person distances. Finishers across all distances received a running t-shirt, a finisher medal, and giveaways, with participants invited to submit times through an online form for a certificate of completion.

The course used the paved trails of Eleanor Tinsley Park and the adjacent Buffalo Bayou path along Allen Parkway, the same downtown-Houston corridor that hosts the Bayou City Classic and many of the city's smaller-field road events.

Saturday's weather in Houston was warm and very humid ahead of an approaching late-day cold front. Space City Weather's April 4 outlook called for showers and thunderstorms to develop northwest of the metro area in the afternoon and move through roughly 4 to 6 PM into the evening, with highly variable rain totals across the region. The morning race windows ran before the unsettled weather arrived. Easter Sunday that followed turned out mostly cloudy and noticeably cooler, with highs only in the mid-60s and lows in the upper 50s.

race_name: Art Car IPA 5K Social Run/Walk at Saint Arnold
title: Art Car IPA social run gathers runners at Saint Arnold ahead of April 12 race
content_md:
The Saint Arnold Art Car IPA 5K held its final pre-race social run/walk and early packet pickup on Saturday morning, April 4, 2026, at Saint Arnold Brewing Company, 2000 Lyons Avenue, Houston. The event served both as a training group for the April 12 Art Car IPA 5K and as a brewery-based social gathering.

Doors opened at 8:00 AM and the run/walk started at 9:00 AM, with Beer Pacers leading participants on an approximately three-mile route. Registration was $35 and included three beers, a commemorative pint glass, and a Texas Tamale Company breakfast. Packet pickup for the main April 12 race opened at 10:00 AM and ran until 2:00 PM. Organizers noted the event would run rain or shine.

Saturday morning conditions in Houston were warm and humid ahead of an approaching late-day cold front. Space City Weather's April 4 outlook called for showers and thunderstorms to develop northwest of the metro area in the afternoon and move through the region roughly 4 to 6 PM into the evening, with highly variable rain totals. The 9:00 AM social run and the extended packet-pickup window both ran before the unsettled weather arrived.

The Art Car IPA 5K social runs have become a regular stop on the Houston spring calendar, pairing a casual, beer-oriented shakeout with early packet distribution for one of the city's larger recreational 5Ks the following weekend.

title: Woodlands Township's Run and Done 3.1 5K runs the Waterway on humid April Saturday
content_md:
The Woodlands Township Parks and Recreation Department hosted its 2026 Run and Done 3.1 5K on Saturday, April 4, 2026, starting and finishing at 2099 Lake Robbins Drive along The Woodlands Waterway. The race ran from 7 to 9 AM and was sanctioned by USA Track & Field.

The course followed the Waterway through The Woodlands Town Center. Awards were presented on site to overall male and female finishers and to first and second place in male and female age groups, with official results to be posted by the township no later than 5:00 PM on Saturday, April 19. Registration and communications for the race were handled through the township's Parks and Recreation office.

Weather across the greater Houston area on Saturday morning was warm and humid ahead of an approaching late-day cold front. Space City Weather's April 4 outlook called for showers and thunderstorms to develop northwest of the metro area in the afternoon and move through roughly 4 to 6 PM into the evening, with highly variable rain totals. The 7 to 9 AM Waterway race window ran well before the active weather arrived. Easter Sunday that followed turned noticeably cooler, with highs in the mid-60s and lows in the upper 50s.

Run and Done 3.1 is part of The Woodlands Township's long-running slate of parks-and-recreation races and remains a recurring fixture on the north-side spring calendar.

title: H-Town Half Marathon sets course records at Minute Maid Park
content_md:
The H-Town Half Marathon, 10K & 5K presented by Houston Methodist took over the streets around Minute Maid Park on Saturday, March 14, 2026, with runners setting new course records across multiple distances.

Aaron Albert blazed the half marathon course in 1:11:49, setting a new men's course record. Christopher Wallace claimed the 5K men's course record in 15:59, while Virginia Wallace set the women's 5K course record at 18:31. The half marathon started at 7:40 AM with the 5K and 10K following at 8:00 AM.

The courses offered scenic views of downtown Houston, running entirely on paved streets and paved trails near the ballpark district. The half marathon carried a 3:30 cutoff and the 5K/10K a 1:30 cutoff, accommodating runners and walkers of all paces.

The H-Town race is part of the Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist, one of the city's premier race series that showcases unique venues throughout the metro area. Award ceremonies followed each race, with the 5K awards at 9:00 AM, 10K at 9:45 AM, and half marathon at 10:15 AM.

title: 49th Bayou City Classic draws 3,000 to downtown Houston as RRCA State Championship
content_md:
The 49th running of the Bayou City Classic 10K and 5K Fun Run returned to Hermann Square adjacent to Houston City Hall on Saturday, March 7, 2026, drawing more than 3,000 runners, walkers, and volunteers to the streets of downtown Houston.

The Bayou City Classic, the oldest 10K in Texas, was selected as the 2026 RRCA State Championship 10K, adding an extra layer of competition to an already storied event. The 10K started at 8:00 AM with the 5K Fun Run/Walk following at 8:20 AM, both courses winding through downtown along Buffalo Bayou.

The flat, fast course that starts and finishes at Sam Houston Park has been a Houston tradition for more than 45 years, making it one of the city's most established running events. The race is organized by the Houston Area Road Runners Association (HARRA) and draws a mix of competitive runners chasing personal records and recreational joggers enjoying the downtown scenery.

Post-race festivities at Hermann Square included awards, food, and the community atmosphere that has kept runners coming back to the Bayou City Classic for nearly five decades.

title: The Woodlands Marathon weekend fills The Woodlands with distance runners
content_md:
The Woodlands Marathon weekend took place on Saturday, March 7, 2026, bringing marathon, half marathon, 10K, 5K, and 2K participants to the scenic streets and trails of The Woodlands, Texas.

The course winds through the master-planned community's tree-lined neighborhoods and along the waterway, offering one of the most spectator-friendly marathon courses in the Houston metro area. The event has grown steadily since its inception, attracting runners from across Texas and beyond who appreciate the flat terrain and well-organized aid stations.

Multiple distances made the weekend accessible to runners of all levels, from first-time 5K participants to experienced marathoners using the race as a final tune-up before spring ultra season. The 2K fun run gave families and young runners a chance to participate in the weekend's festivities.

Results were posted via MyChipTime, with full marathon, half marathon, 10K, and 5K standings available on the official race website. The Woodlands Marathon has established itself as one of the premier early-spring distance events in the greater Houston area.

title: Inaugural Houston Roast Run pairs 5K with live comedy along Buffalo Bayou
content_md:
The inaugural Houston Roast Run & Comedy Show hit the Buffalo Bayou trails on Saturday, March 7, 2026, combining a 5K run with a post-race stand-up comedy show at The Water Works at Buffalo Bayou.

The 5K started at 5:00 PM — an unusual late-afternoon start time — and featured professional comedians stationed along the course armed with microphones, roasting runners' strides, outfits, and life choices as they passed. The format was a first for Houston's running scene, blending fitness with live entertainment in a way that drew both runners and comedy fans.

After crossing the finish line, participants gathered for a full comedy show at 6:30 PM featuring four of Houston's top comedians. The event offered multiple ticket options including a 5K-only, comedy-show-only, and combined packages.

Originally scheduled for January, the race was postponed due to Houston's February freeze and rescheduled to March 7. The event is produced by a new Houston race company entering a crowded local market with a distinctive format that sets it apart from the city's traditional 5K scene.

title: Over 12,000 runners fill downtown Houston streets for 2026 Rodeo Run
content_md:
The 2026 Rodeo Run presented by ConocoPhillips filled the streets of downtown Houston on Saturday, February 28, with more than 12,000 runners and walkers turning out for one of the city's largest annual running events.

The race started at the corner of Walker and Bagby and finished on Allen Parkway near Eleanor Tinsley Park, where participants gathered for the post-race celebration. The event offered 5K, 10K, 10K Wheelchair, and a Sleep-In option for those who preferred to support the cause without running.

Trophies were awarded to overall male and female winners in the 5K, 10K, and wheelchair divisions, as well as masters champions and the top three finishers in each age group. The Rodeo Run is one of Houston's signature community events, drawing a cross-section of competitive runners, casual joggers, and families united by the energy of Houston Livestock Show and Rodeo season.

All proceeds benefited the Houston Livestock Show and Rodeo's educational initiatives in Texas, which have awarded more than $600 million in scholarships and grants since 1932.

title: Katy Half Marathon draws runners to Typhoon Texas for Run Houston series race two
content_md:
The Katy Half Marathon & 5K presented by Houston Methodist took place on Sunday, February 8, 2026, with start and finish at Typhoon Texas Waterpark Houston at 555 Katy Fort Bend Road.

The event is the second race in the Run Houston Race Series and offered a half marathon, timed and untimed 5K, and Kids 1K. The flat Katy-area course is known for producing fast times, with event records of 1:09:29 (men's half, set in 2015) and 1:20:07 (women's half, set in 2019) standing as benchmarks for elite local runners.

The Katy Half Marathon has become a key early-season race for Houston-area runners building toward spring marathons and half marathons. The February date provides a mid-training-cycle checkpoint for those targeting the Woodlands Marathon in March or spring trail races at Brazos Bend.

As part of the Run Houston series, the event supported community nonprofits focused on providing shoes and socks to underserved children, continuing the series' charitable mission across all nine of its annual events.

title: Habtom Samuel shatters course record with 59:01 debut at Aramco Houston Half Marathon
content_md:
Eritrea's Habtom Samuel stunned the running world with a 59:01 course record at the Aramco Houston Half Marathon on Sunday, January 11, 2026 — his debut at the distance. The University of New Mexico student-athlete and reigning NCAA Cross Country Champion stayed patient through the early miles before surging to the lead with a mile to go.

In the women's race, Ethiopia's Fentaye Belayneh, the African 5000m champion, pulled clear in the closing stages and crossed the line in 1:04:49, taking more than two minutes off her personal best. Gebreselama finished second in 1:04:52, with Diriba third in 1:05:57 and USA's Taylor Roe fourth in 1:06:20.

The half marathon ran concurrently with the Chevron Houston Marathon on the familiar downtown Houston course, starting and finishing at the George R. Brown Convention Center. Houston's flat course and typically cool January conditions have long made it a destination for fast half marathon times, and the 2026 edition delivered on that reputation with Samuel's sub-60 debut.

The race is part of the World Athletics Label Road Race series, and the combined marathon weekend drew tens of thousands of participants to downtown Houston.

title: We Are Houston 5K kicks off marathon weekend in downtown Houston
content_md:
The We Are Houston 5K presented by Aramco and Chevron opened the 2026 Houston Marathon weekend on Saturday morning, January 10, with runners gathering at McKinney and Crawford in downtown Houston for a 6:30 AM start.

The 5K serves as the community-friendly kickoff to the marathon weekend, drawing a mix of casual runners, families, and marathon participants shaking out their legs before Sunday's main events. The flat course winds through downtown streets before returning to the start/finish area near the George R. Brown Convention Center.

The race is part of the broader Chevron Houston Marathon weekend that also includes the full marathon and the Aramco Houston Half Marathon the following day. For many participants, the We Are Houston 5K is their introduction to the Houston Marathon weekend experience, with the energy of thousands of runners preparing for the next day's longer distances adding to the electric atmosphere.

race_name: Run Houston! Sam Houston Race Park 5K & 10K
title: Run Houston series opens 2026 season at Sam Houston Race Park
content_md:
The Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist kicked off its 2026 season with the Sam Houston Race Park 5K & 10K on Saturday, January 3, 2026, at the horse racing venue in northwest Houston.

The flat, fast out-and-back course started and finished in the Sam Houston Race Park parking lot, with the 5K and 10K beginning at 9:30 AM followed by a Kids 1K at 10:20 AM. Award ceremonies for both distances took place at 10:00 AM.

The Sam Houston Race Park event is the first of nine races in the Run Houston series, which showcases unique venues throughout the Houston metro area. The series partners with community nonprofits to provide sneakers and socks to underserved children and teens, giving participants a charitable purpose alongside their fitness goals.

The venue offered free parking and a straightforward logistics experience, with last-minute registration and packet pickup available from 7:15 to 8:30 AM on race morning.

title: Chevron Houston Marathon returns to downtown under ideal January conditions
content_md:
Runners gathered in downtown Houston on Sunday, January 19, 2025 for the Chevron Houston Marathon, one of the largest marathons in the United States and a qualifier for both the Boston Marathon and the U.S. Olympic Marathon Trials.

The race started and finished at the George R. Brown Convention Center, winding through downtown, Midtown, the Heights, and Memorial Park on a flat course that is popular with runners chasing personal-best times. Weather on race morning was cool and calm, conditions long considered ideal for distance running in the city.

Crowd support along Heights Boulevard and through Memorial is a trademark of the event, with front yards and neighborhood corners becoming impromptu aid stations and cheering sections. The companion Aramco Houston Half Marathon ran simultaneously on an abbreviated route.

After the finish, participants gathered in the convention center's post-race food hall for the hot broth, grilled cheese, and oversized medals that have become annual traditions at the Chevron-sponsored event.
